assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md contains: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812 (line 7), Vulnerable library = quinn-proto (line 14), Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (i.e., < 0.11.14)' (line 15), Fixed version = 0.11.14 (line 16). All four fields match the source Vulnerability issue TC-8001.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 39: 'Ecosystem: Cargo'. Line 38: 'Library: quinn-proto (Rust crate)'. Line 40: 'Lock file: Cargo.lock'. The ecosystem detection correctly identifies quinn-proto as a Cargo (Rust) dependency.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: security-matrix-mock.md defines 7 versions across two streams: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (stream 2.1.x) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (stream 2.2.x). version-impact.md table lists all 7 versions: 2.1.0 (v0.3.8), 2.1.1 (v0.3.12), 2.2.0 (v0.4.5), 2.2.1 (v0.4.8), 2.2.2 (v0.4.8), 2.2.3 (v0.4.11), 2.2.4 (v0.4.12). No versions are missing.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Pinned Tag column matches security-matrix-mock.md exactly: 2.1.0=v0.3.8, 2.1.1=v0.3.12, 2.2.0=v0.4.5, 2.2.1=v0.4.8, 2.2.2=v0.4.8, 2.2.3=v0.4.11, 2.2.4=v0.4.12. Line 21-22 states: 'the quinn-proto dependency version was extracted from Cargo.lock at the pinned backend commit tag listed in the supportability matrix'. No branch HEAD references are used for released versions.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 15: '2.2.2 | 2.2.x | v0.4.8 | -- | YES | retag of 2.2.1 (same backend tag v0.4.8); result carried forward'. Line 27: 'Version 2.2.2 is a retag of 2.2.1 -- both use backend tag v0.4.8. The lock file check was skipped for 2.2.2 and the result (quinn-proto 0.11.12, AFFECTED) was carried forward from 2.2.1.' This matches Important Rule 5 exactly.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 9-12 show an explicit diff block: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.0.0]' vs 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. The rationale table (lines 16-23) shows lock-file-verified evidence for each version's inclusion/exclusion.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses version names throughout: 'RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2' (line 12). The proposed Jira mutation (lines 42-48) uses placeholder IDs '<RHTPA-2.2.0-jira-id>' etc. Line 50 explicitly states: 'Jira version IDs (shown as placeholders above) would be discovered dynamically at runtime via getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields for the Vulnerability issue type. The version names (RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2) are matched against the allowedValues returned by the API to resolve their IDs.' No hardcoded numeric IDs are present.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 (upstream): Repository = rhtpa-backend (line 29), Target Branch = release/0.4.z (line 33), Acceptance Criteria with checkboxes (lines 54-56), Dependencies = TC-8001 (line 65). Task 2 (downstream): Repository = rhtpa-release.0.4.z (line 79), Target Branch = main (line 83), Acceptance Criteria with checkboxes (lines 98-99), Dependencies = upstream backport task + TC-8001 (lines 108-109). Linkage: Blocks link between upstream and downstream tasks (lines 150-153).

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md line 5-6: 'Since quinn-proto is a Cargo ecosystem (source dependency), two tasks are required: 1. Upstream backport task, 2. Downstream propagation subtask'. Line 10: 'The downstream subtask is Blocks-linked to the upstream task'. Task 1 (line 20): 'PROPOSAL: Task 1 -- Upstream Backport Task'. Task 2 (line 69): 'PROPOSAL: Task 2 -- Downstream Propagation Subtask'. Blocks link (lines 150-153): 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream_task.key>, outwardIssue: <downstream_task.key>, type: "Blocks")'.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: All Jira mutations are prefixed with 'PROPOSAL:' throughout the outputs. affects-versions.md line 41: 'PROPOSAL: jira.edit_issue("TC-8001", ...)'; line 55: 'PROPOSAL: jira.add_comment("TC-8001", ...)'. remediation.md line 115: 'All operations below are proposals requiring engineer confirmation before execution.' Lines 120, 126, 138, 144, 150 all begin with 'PROPOSAL:'. No output indicates any mutation was actually executed.
